notes_md:
**Khen** = to praise, to compliment someone for something good. It’s what you say when someone does well or has a good quality.

It’s a verb, so you usually **khen + someone** for an action or quality.  

You can khen someone’s skills, behavior, looks, work, etc. The opposite is **chê** (to criticize), which makes a nice memory pair: *khen ↔ chê*.

content.sentence: Cô ấy rất ___ về thành tích của bạn.

translation.en: She is very praising about your achievements.

definitions.vi: Diễn đạt sự tán thưởng hoặc ngưỡng mộ đối với ai đó hoặc điều gì đó.
definitions.es: Expresar aprobación o admiración por alguien o algo.
definitions.en: To express approval or admiration for someone or something.

example.vi: Cô ấy được khen vì đã làm việc chăm chỉ.
example.en: She is praised for working hard.
example.es: Ella es elogiada por trabajar duro.

notes:
Khen = to praise, to compliment someone for something good. It’s what you say when someone does well or has a good quality.

It’s a verb, so you usually khen + someone for an action or quality.  

You can khen someone’s skills, behavior, looks, work, etc. The opposite is chê (to criticize), which makes a nice memory pair: khen ↔ chê.

content.sentence: Tôi muốn ___ bạn vì sự nỗ lực.

translation.en: I want to praise you for the effort.

exampleSentences: Sếp đã khen anh vì hoàn thành dự án đúng hạn.
Bố mẹ luôn khen em khi em học giỏi ở trường.
Bạn bè thường khen chị về khả năng nấu ăn ngon.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The boss gave praise to me for finishing the project on time.
My parents always give praise to me when I do well at school.
Friends often give praise to her for her good cooking skills.
